wikidata_candidates.label: Richard Butsch
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The card describes an engineer and researcher at Esso Research & Engineering with technical expertise in civil engineering, hydraulics, and environmental simulation. The Wikidata entity refers to a Richard Butsch who is a professor; this Wikidata ID (Q112408426) typically identifies the sociologist and media historian Richard Butsch. The professional fields (engineering/physics vs. sociology/media studies) are incompatible, indicating they are two different individuals with the same name.

results.possibleIdentity: Richard J. Butsch was a Research Engineer specializing in environmental control and hydraulics during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by the Esso Research and Engineering Company, based in Florham Park, New Jersey (near Morristown). His work focused on the application of mathematical optimization techniques, such as linear and dynamic programming, to civil engineering and environmental systems. In 1970, he published a significant paper titled "Reservoir System Design Optimization" in the Journal of the Hydraulics Division of the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE), where he was an Associate Member. His expertise encompassed civil engineering, physics, hydraulics, computer programming, and the simulation of environmental and systems models. He is noted as a potential participant in Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that matched technical professionals from companies like Esso with artists for collaborative projects.
